# testdata

Checked-in fixtures for black-box API tests, resolved via
`testharness.TestdataPath(...)`.

Convention (established here, reused by every later slice):

- Each package that needs fixtures gets its own `testdata/` tree.
- Media fixtures (later: tiny real clips for ffprobe direct-play vs
  transcode-required decisions per the PRD) live under
  `testdata/media/<library-name>/...` mirroring the on-disk naming convention.
- Tests arrange a fixture tree + a temp data dir, drive the server over HTTP,
  and assert on responses and observable state — never on internal module
  shapes or the SQLite schema.

## movies/ (issue 04 — core identity)

Three clean fixtures (`Dune (2021)`, `Blade Runner (1982)`, bare
`Sample Movie (2000).mp4`) exercising the single-file → one-Title-one-Edition
path. Regenerated by `catalog_test.go`'s `TestMain`/`ensureFixtures` if missing.

## naming/ (issue 05 — full naming convention)

A second Library root exercising the full Movie naming convention, asserted in
`naming_test.go`. Kept separate from `movies/` so the core three-Title
regression is untouched. `ensureNamingFixtures` regenerates any missing clip or
poster with ffmpeg (lazily, via package `init`); the tests skip if ffmpeg is
absent.

| Fixture | Exercises |
|---|---|
| `Edition Movie (2010)/… - 1080p.mkv` + `… - 2160p.mkv` | distinct quality tokens → two Editions |
| `Cut Movie (2011)/… {edition-Director's Cut}.mp4` | explicit named Edition |
| `Split Movie (2012)/… - part1.mp4` + `… - part2.mp4` | multi-part → one Edition, two Files |
| `Extras Movie (2013)/` main + `-trailer` + `Trailers/teaser` + `sample.mp4` + `poster.jpg`/`fanart.jpg` | extras attached & hidden; junk ignored; local artwork |
| `Pinned Movie (2014) {tmdb-12345}/…` | embedded id recorded + used as identity |
| `Yearless Movie/…` | partial parse → filed + needs-review |
| `1080p.mkv` (bare, quality-token-only name) | no minimal identity → Unmatched |
